definition: Knowing arising dependent on the body and tangibles.
notes: The project prefers body-knowing because it stays close to the canonical sixfold formula while aligning with the updated treatment of viññāṇa as knowing. Body-consciousness remains an acceptable alternate where continuity with familiar doctrinal English matters. The OSF glossary confirms body-knowing as the house default for this viññāṇa compound.

context_rules.context: In most analytical and source-facing contexts
In explanatory prose emphasizing the tactile mode
context_rules.notes: Use this as a controlled alternate.

context_rules.context: In comparative doctrinal prose using established Buddhist-English terminology
context_rules.notes: Use this as a controlled alternate when continuity with standard terminology helps.

example_phrases.pali: kāyañca paṭicca phoṭṭhabbe ca uppajjati kāyaviññāṇaṃ
example_phrases.translation: dependent on the body and tangibles, body-knowing arises

authority_basis.scope: Supports keeping body-knowing as the house default.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use 'body-knowing' as the default rendering in the entry's governed translation contexts unless a recorded context rule overrides it.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Yield to recorded context_rules, source-facing technical use, or other explicitly documented local reasons rather than rotating among alternates ad hoc.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents drift back toward 'touch-consciousness' or other unrecorded alternates.
